item.title: Anorectal fibrosis in 3 murine models of chronic intestinal fibrosis: a comparative analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Anorectal fibrosis is a poorly understood condition of Crohn's disease (CD)-associated perianal complications, characterized by transmural fibrotic remodeling that may contribute to luminal narrowi...

item.title: Granzyme K CD8⁺ T cells with tissue-resident features promote intestinal inflammation in patients with Crohn's disease.
item.description: Experimental & molecular medicine - The role of CD8+ T cells in Crohn's disease (CD) pathogenesis remains incompletely understood. This study aimed to characterize CD8+ T cells in CD and elucidate their potential contribution to inte...

item.title: Exploring the body image experiences of women with a permanent stoma.
item.description: Journal of health psychology - Stoma surgery can bring significant physical and psychological changes to an individual's life. This study explored the experiences of living with a stoma and body image. 12 semi-structured intervi...

item.title: Peritoneal Tuberculosis Complicated by Abdominal Abscesses: Diagnostic Challenges and Clinical Insights From Two Cases.
item.description: Journal of investigative medicine high impact case reports - Case report - Peritoneal tuberculosis (PT) is a rare form of extrapulmonary Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection that may closely mimic Crohn's disease (CD) or intra-abdominal malignancy, leading to diagnostic d...

item.title: Beyond Incontinence: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Sexual Dysfunction after Anal Fistula Surgery.
item.description: Journal of gastrointestinal surgery : official journal of the Society for Surgery of the Alimentary Tract - Review - Traditional metrics for anal fistula surgery prioritise healing and continence, largely ignoring intimacy and psychosocial well-being. Addressing a critical literature gap, this novel systematic re...

item.title: Safety and efficacy of strictureplasty for primary proximal duodenal Crohn's disease: a retrospective single-center case series.
item.description: Updates in surgery - Primary proximal duodenal Crohn's disease (CD) is a rare entity that can lead to fibrotic strictures and gastric outlet obstruction. Surgical intervention is often required in refractory cases, yet...

item.title: Single-center experience with robotic colorectal surgery for benign indications.
item.description: International journal of colorectal disease - Robotic colorectal surgery is established for malignant indications, but evidence supporting its use in benign disease remains limited. We describe perioperative outcomes of 403 consecutive robotic...

item.title: Evaluating the Role of Magnetic Resonance Imaging in the Surgical Management of Perianal Fistula.
item.description: The Journal of surgical research - Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is increasingly recommended by international opinion for the evaluation of perianal fistula, but whether MRI is associated with improved operative outcomes remains ...

item.title: Patients with Ileal Pouch-Anal Anastomosis Have Decreased Bowel Frequency on Glucagon-like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonist Therapy.
item.description: Clinical and translational gastroenterology - High bowel frequency after ileal pouch-anal anastomosis (IPAA) causes significant symptom burden. We aimed to evaluate the impact of GLP-1 receptor agonists (GLP1RAs) in this setting. We conducted ...

item.title: Delayed Loop Ileostomy Reversal Increases the Risk of Colitis and Clostridium difficile Infection.
item.description: ANZ journal of surgery - Temporary loop ileostomy formation is a routine procedure performed to protect distal colorectal anastomosis. Traditionally, the ideal timing of ileostomy reversal is believed to fall between 3 and...

item.title: Challenges in Diagnosis and Management of Crohn's Disease in Bangladesh With Long Term Follow-Up.
item.description: Clinical case reports - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic autoimmune inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) with significant diagnostic and therapeutic challenges. We reported a case of a 20-year-old Bangladeshi male with an 8-...

item.title: High and low body mass index and 90-day postoperative outcomes in patients with Crohn's disease undergoing abdominal surgery.
item.description: American journal of surgery - To evaluate the impact of high and low Body mass index (BMI) on 90-day postoperative outcomes in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). This retrospective cohort study (2017-2019) included 271 patient...

item.title: Visual theranostic oral nanozymes for IBD from principles to preclinical design.
item.description: Bioactive materials - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), which includes ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is a chronic, relapsing inflammatory disorder of the intestine driven by disruption of mucosal imm...
